title: Cubit
objectives: Explain Cubit as a simpler BLoC

sections.markdown: A Cubit is a lighter form of BLoC from the same package: instead of events, you call methods that emit new states directly. Less boilerplate, same testability and immutable-state model.
Use Cubit by default; reach for BLoC when you need the explicit event trail (auditing/debugging complex flows) or advanced event transformers (debounce, throttle, concurrency control).
Cubit and BLoC share the same widgets (BlocProvider/BlocBuilder/BlocListener) and the same testing tools, so migrating Cubit → BLoC later is straightforward.

flashcards.back: Cubit emits states from method calls; BLoC maps explicit events to states.
When you need an event trail or transformers (debounce/throttle/concurrency).

flashcards.front: Do Cubit and BLoC share widgets?

quiz.q: Choose BLoC over Cubit when you need…

options: Share the same widgets and tests
quiz.explain: Same package, same UI widgets and testing tools.
